mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, deteriorating labor market conditions trigger recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling real personal savings rates indicates aggressive consumption drawdowns to sustain demand, which historically precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up, RSAFS down) fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of credit contraction and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not produce statistically significant negative 60-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, compared to periods of stable or falling rates.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings bases, directly supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates above trend fail to correlate with positive 60-day forward SPX returns (correlation coefficient < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 3.5% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, suppressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in >60% of instances where CPI annualized growth exceeds 3.5% for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in payroll employment and contraction in real personal savings rates indicates labor market overheating coupled with depleted household buffers, which triggers sharp earnings revisions and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling RSAFS does not predict negative 5-day forward returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 55%.

mechanism_narrative: In a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment, a sustained increase in industrial production alongside stable or rising CPI indicates strong pricing power and healthy demand absorption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising INDPRO combined with stable or rising CPI fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns in >60% of observed instances.
